programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: For the first time cellphone data made its way to the jury. On the stand was a digital forensics examiner who extracted Jade Janks's phone at the time prosecutors alleged she killed Tom Merriman. A detective in the district attorney's office took the stand. He was involved in interviewing one of three men Janks allegedly tried to get to help her kill her stepfather, who lived next door to her.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: In her murder trial, Jade Janks's lead attorney had questions for the detective who conducted interviews during the murder investigation of Tom Merriman. Detective Scott Stallman interviewed one of the guys Janks allegedly recruited to help her kill her stepfather. Next, the lead detective, Matthew Gibson, testified to his work in this case. This would be the state's final witness to jurors in their case-in-chief.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: The Work Of The Lead Detective
programme.desc: The prescription medication that Tom Merriman was taking was on display for jurors.

programme.desc: Police showed up to Alex Woodworth's house the day he was killed. The defendant in this case, Ezra McCandless, happened to be at the house. Her ex-boyfriend, Jason, noticed Ezra was off and looking very agitated.

programme.title: Killer Girlfriend Murder Trial

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1986, police struggle to find a young mother's killer. Years later, can genetic genealogy provide answers?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1990 Mississippi, a woman is kidnapped and raped at knifepoint. Police are unable to identify the perpetrator. It'll take forward-thinking investigators, state-of-the-art science and brave survivors to put this man behind bars.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: This episode follows an extensive investigation and how the revolutionary science of familial DNA revealed a perpetrator's identity.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Michelle Wyatt is found strangled to death in 1980. Few leads arise until 41 years later, thanks to forensics.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In Sacramento, three women are assaulted by the same man. Police use genealogy technology to find the suspect.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.desc: The episode follows the investigation of uncovering a serial killer and how DNA genetic genealogy solved the murders.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1990, 28-year-old Genevieve Zitricki is brutally murdered in her Greenville, South Carolina apartment. The case goes cold until DNA technology links her murder to a serial offender.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1987, the body of Darlene Krashoc is discovered behind a Colorado Springs restaurant. She had been savagely beaten, raped and strangled. Investigators struggle to identify this barbaric killer. 30 years later, science provides a breakthrough.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: DNA and genealogy reveal the identity of a man responsible for violent home invasions from 1982 to 1997.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: He was a recluse who spread terror through the mail for 18 years, but Ted Kaczynski wasn't known as the Unabomber until his brother tipped off the FBI. Kaczynski's manifesto, published in major newspapers, set off a flurry of activity for the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Washington DC, several officers had been sitting in their patrol cars when a man opened fire on them.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a Washington, DC professor was shot and killed as she tried to get into her car, the latest incident in a series of violent carjackings. One week later, a man robbed a bank and security cameras captured the same vehicle as the getaway car.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The decapitation of an African-American male led the Miami-Dade police and the FBI along a trail of murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In New York, an armored car robbery left one guard dead and another critically wounded. The FBI suspected domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. Their investigation led them to the Black Liberation Army and the Weather Underground.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, two men used a sledgehammer to break into a Camden, New Jersey home. Police tracked the suspects to a nearby park but the criminals were able to escape after a nearly fatal shootout.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The True Story of Mississippi Burning
programme.desc: In 1964, civil rights workers who set out to educate Southern Black people about the right to vote disappeared in Mississippi. After they were found lynched, the FBI gained the trust of an informant who led to the conviction of local KKK members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: For two couples on vacation, an island paradise turned to hell when one couple murders the other. Eleven years after the murder, a drifter sailing the high seas to escape a drug charge was convicted of the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1989, a quiet community in Ohio was shattered when a sniper gunned down an outdoorsman without reason.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In December 1981, a New Jersey state trooper was shot to death on the side of Interstate 80. Fingerprints on the suspect's vehicle pointed to a fugitive wanted on bank robbery charges.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When an 80-year-old woman is found barely conscious next to the body of her murdered son in an abandoned van, the FBI was brought in and linked the crime to a teenager and middle-aged man.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a couple swept through six states like a plague. They targeted adults and young girls for kidnap and murder. The gruesome crime spree left eight people dead, so the FBI launched a manhunt for killers Alton Coleman and Debra Denise Brown.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The FBI investigated the Dixie Mafia after the 1987 murder-for-hire killing of a circuit court judge.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1995, a South Carolina family was taken hostage as part of a bank robbery. Local police quickly identified two of the perpetrators but the criminal mastermind behind it all proved to be more elusive.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a killer so evil that not even her own son could escape her clutches. She became the first woman to be executed in Florida since 1848.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Billy Mansfield Jr. moved between Florida and Michigan, targeting vulnerable women and girls. Between 1975 and 1980, Mansfield Jr. murdered at least five victims, burying four of them at his family home in Spring Hill, Florida.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Runge raped and killed 6 women and a young girl in Chicago, Illinois. The Police had suspected him in the early investigation but lacked the evidence to make an arrest.

programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.desc: When Carol Jarvis disappeared, her husband told friends and family that she'd simply gone on a little holiday.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jerome Brudos was a serial killer and necrophile who murdered four women in Oregon during the 1960s. He was known as the 'The Lust Killer' and 'The Shoe Fetish Slayer.'

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Timothy Wilson Spencer was identified as the man who raped and strangled five young women in Virginia. With the use of DNA profiling, an innocent man is freed and the real killer is convicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a hit-and-run accident claimed the life of a high school athlete, everyone in town mourned his passing. Finding the killer was a long shot at best.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A brilliant young architect became ill and died just before she was to testify in a criminal trial. The autopsy revealed she'd been poisoned with arsenic. Investigators had to determine which person who knew her had a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a pipe bomb rips through a rural home, killing a young man and seriously injuring his mother, detectives hope that the remnants of a mailing label will lead them to a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2005, David Castor suffered a slow, agonizing death over a period of days. His wife maintained it was suicide, even though it was done with antifreeze.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two women from the same town were murdered in the same way, police feared a serial killer was on the loose. At first they thought the victims had nothing in common until they found tiny clues linking them to the same man.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of a World War II veteran slowed to a halt when the prime suspect had a solid alibi. But a lucky break led to a shady character who wore distinctive boots.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, 17-month-old Josh Hinson died in a fire which a federal agency ruled was intentionally set by Josh's mother Terri Strickland. Using the then-emerging resources of the Internet, Terri Strickland undertook her own investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A girl claimed she had been abducted. She recounted what happened but things didn't add up to police.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How did the stalker obtain the security system code for his victim's home? How did he steal her personal photographs? Police needed answers and they found them in the most unlikely of places.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In this international case of extortion, murder and stolen identities, scam-artist Albert J. Walker assumes the identity of co-worker Ronald Platt.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1981, six-year-old Cassie Hansen disappeared from her St. Paul, Minnesota church during an evening service.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2003, Tiffany Rowell and three of her friends were brutally murdered in affluent Clear Lake, Texas.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bombings are difficult to solve, because the perpetrator isn't usually at the scene, and the evidence goes up in smoke. In this case pieces of plastic the size of grains of sand hold the key to a man's murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Responding to a 2008 garage fire, Illinois emergency response discovered a man crushed beneath a truck.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When DNA proves that a man who practically admits to a brutal attack is innocent, police wonder why he is willing to take the blame.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial rapist is on the loose in a Texas town. No one knew where he'd strike next, but the audacity of his attacks and the escalation of violence against his victims made finding him imperative.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three homicides on two continents looked like professional executions. Investigators on both sides of the Atlantic needed to find out if they were related and, if they were, who or what they had in common.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For more than a year, hateful letters were sent to a school teacher in Pennsylvania. DNA analysis would eventually help seal the perpetrator's fate.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a hit-and-run boating accident caused a death, police must search for one boat among 1200 others.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One investigator's quick thinking helps him capture a shoe impression made in snow before it melts away.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Susie Mowbray was charged for her husband Bill Mowbray's death, but her son was convinced of her innocence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1987, the death of Crystal Purcell was considered an accident. Then in 2001, Barbara Purcell called police to suggest that her estranged husband had killed Crystal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspicious fire swept through an apartment killing two young women. A closer look at the fire scene revealed something hidden in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police led an intense search and relied on forensics when the mother of two young children went missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the early hours of Christmas Eve, a college co-ed was abducted from the parking lot of her apartment.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the Amtrak Railroad experienced the deadliest train crash in United States history when the Sunset Limited derailed while crossing Alabama's Big Bayou Canot bridge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A murder trail turned cold, until police got a call from a woman whose husband, Gerald Powers, had a criminal past and a fondness for Chevy Berettas.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A six-year-old girl ran and hid when she saw her grandmother being beaten to death, but the man followed, beat and assaulted her. She said the assailant was her uncle, who was convicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A body is found stuffed into a suitcase forcing the police to search though the landfill for another missing victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men were murdered in their beds. The next night, arson destroyed a family planning clinic. Investigators find a link between the two separate crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A car carrying three men pulls up alongside another on an Alaskan highway and fires shots, leaving a passenger dead. One of the passengers in the killer's car agrees to testify against his friends. The resulting trials don't end the carnage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, California firefighters battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the case to similar fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police arrested a suspect for the death of a girl. But new technology proved the wrong man was behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A driver said he couldn't have hit and killed a pedestrian because his Jeep had been sold months ago.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of an attractive young woman was found a mile from her abandoned car. Police were especially concerned when they realized the victim had come to them for protection just two weeks earlier.
